titleEn: Beef drives 40% of global agriculture deforestation
titleZh: 牛肉生产驱动全球40%农业相关森林砍伐
summaryZh: 瑞典查尔姆斯理工大学研究显示，2001-2022年间牛肉生产占农业相关森林砍伐的40%，巴西是最大贡献国（32%）。研究覆盖184种商品、179个国家，指出主食作物（玉米、水稻、木薯）合计占11%，超过可可、咖啡等出口产品。全球共损失1.21亿公顷森林，排放412亿吨二氧化碳当量。该研究为智慧畜牧行业提供了可持续牧场管理与供应链追溯的宏观背景与数据支撑。
featuredReason: 该研究揭示了牛肉生产在全球森林砍伐中的主导地位，为智慧畜牧行业提供了可持续牧场管理、精准养殖与供应链追溯的宏观需求背景，是推动行业绿色转型的关键数据参考。
contentFull: Herd Management Sustainable Practices Beef drives 40% of global agriculture deforestation Staple crops outpace export goods in forest loss share 26 March 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media Europe Beef production is the leading driver of agriculture-linked deforestation, accounting for 40% of all forest clearing done to open space for food production, Reuters reported, citing details of a study released on Tuesday.Brazil, the world's largest exporter of beef and soybeans, is topping the list of countries that have cleared the most forests to expand agriculture, the study said.Researchers from Chalmers University of Technology in Sweden studied 184 agricultural commodities across 179 countries from 2001-2022, using a model combining satellite data and agricultural statistics to produce what they describe as the most comprehensive global survey of agriculture-linked deforestation to date.After beef, the data show palm oil accounting for 9% of global deforestation, soybeans at 5%, maize and rice each at 4%, cassava at 3%, cocoa at 2%, and coffee and rubber at 1% each.On a country level, Brazil was responsible for almost a third of global deforestation (32%) during the period, followed by Indonesia at 9%, China and the Democratic Republic of Congo each at 6%, the US at 5%, and Ivory Coast at 3%.In total, 121 million hectares (299 million acres) of forest were lost between 2001 and 2022, resulting in emissions of 41.2 gigatons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e).The study noted that staple crops — maize, rice and cassava — together account for 11% of agriculture-driven deforestation, more than export products such as cocoa, coffee and rubber combined, and their impact is spread across the globe rather than concentrated in specific regions.Martin Persson, one of the researchers behind the project, said the problem extends beyond trade, indicating that action is also required at producer countries, where domestic agricultural markets drive significant forest loss.Although being one important source of greenhouse gases, deforestation for agriculture accounts for only around 5% of the world's total carbon dioxide emissions, the study said.The researchers plan to expand the model next to include the mining and energy sectors. translationZh:
牛肉生产是农业相关森林砍伐的首要驱动因素，占为开辟粮食生产空间而进行的全部森林砍伐的40%，路透社援引周二发布的一项研究细节报道称。

该研究称，巴西作为全球最大的牛肉和大豆出口国，在因扩大农业而砍伐森林最多的国家名单中位居榜首。

瑞典查尔姆斯理工大学的研究人员利用结合卫星数据和农业统计数据的模型，研究了2001年至2022年间179个国家的184种农业商品，得出了他们所称的迄今为止最全面的全球农业相关森林砍伐调查结果。

继牛肉之后，数据显示棕榈油占全球森林砍伐的9%，大豆占5%，玉米和水稻各占4%，木薯占3%，可可占2%，咖啡和橡胶各占1%。

在国家层面，巴西在此期间占全球森林砍伐的近三分之一（32%），其次是印度尼西亚（9%）、中国和刚果民主共和国（各占6%）、美国（5%）以及科特迪瓦（3%）。

总体而言，2001年至2022年间，共有1.21亿公顷（2.99亿英亩）森林消失，导致412亿吨二氧化碳当量（CO2e）的排放。

研究指出，主食作物——玉米、水稻和木薯——合计占农业驱动森林砍伐的11%，超过了可可、咖啡和橡胶等出口产品的总和，且它们的影响遍布全球，而非集中在特定区域。

该项目的研究人员之一马丁·佩尔松表示，问题不仅限于贸易，还表明在生产者国家也需要采取行动，这些国家的国内农业市场导致了大量森林损失。

该研究称，尽管农业用地的森林砍伐是温室气体的一个重要来源，但它仅占全球二氧化碳总排放量的约5%。

研究人员计划下一步将该模型扩展到采矿和能源领域。
contentHtml:
Beef drives 40% of global agriculture deforestation 
Staple crops outpace export goods in forest loss share
